data.title: Viking Therapeutics stock rises after earnings release, posts $1.38 loss per share. R&D spending jumps to $153.5M from $31M. VK2735 obesity drug progresses in Phase 3 program.
data.description: The stock of Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ: VKTX) is surging, following a positive after-hours session on Wednesday post the release of its fourth-quarter earnings report. The increase in momentum continued on Thursday, although there has been only limited movement in Viking Therapeutics shares. The company reported a loss of $1.38 per share, which was below analysts' expectations of an 89 cent loss. This decline in performance is attributed to a significant rise in research and development expenditure, soaring to $153.5 million from $31 million a year earlier. Despite missing the EPS estimate by 13 cents, CEO Brian Lian highlighted the substantial progress made across the obesity portfolio in the past year. Viking's Phase 3 VANQUISH program for VK2735, their subcutaneous obesity drug, is advancing rapidly towards both injectable and oral formulations. This development indicates positive growth prospects for VKTX stock in the future.

data.title: University of Denver launches Center for Housing Research with $2.65 million support, focuses on housing challenges.
data.description: In Denver on February 12, 2026, the University of Denver (DU) announced the establishment of the Center for Housing Research and Innovative Solutions (CHRIS). This new initiative aims to tackle the area's significant housing issues through research, policy analysis, and educational efforts. The center will serve as a focal point for addressing pressing housing challenges in the region, highlighting the university's commitment to finding solutions for this critical issue.

data.title: BODYBAR Pilates experiences successful growth in 2025 with record studio growth, sales, and franchise demand.
data.description: In 2025, a boutique Pilates franchise based in Fort Worth, Texas, experienced unprecedented growth, achieving record numbers in studio openings, sales, and franchise interest. BODYBAR Pilates, known for its innovative approach to Pilates, saw a surge in demand, indicating a successful year of expansion and performance. This growth trend showcases the significant impact and popularity of the franchise, demonstrating its strong presence in the fitness market.

data.title: Tony Castro introduces MotoTable, a new motorcycle camping workstation, revolutionizing outdoor experiences in Santa Clara, California.
data.description: In Santa Clara, California, Tony Castro has introduced the MotoTable, a groundbreaking motorcycle camping workstation. This innovative product aims to enhance outdoor experiences for motorcycle enthusiasts by providing a comprehensive solution for camping needs. The MotoTable offers a unique design that combines functionality and convenience, catering to the growing demand for versatile outdoor equipment. With a focus on practicality and efficiency, the MotoTable is set to revolutionize the camping industry.

data.title: Churchill Stateside Group secures $1.43M USDA loan for 68-unit senior housing in Castle Hayne, NC.
data.description: Churchill Stateside Group recently finalized a $1.43 million loan through the USDA for a senior housing community in Castle Hayne, NC. The loan will support the development of a 68-unit affordable housing complex designed for senior residents.

data.title: Demetree Global in Winter Park expands storage facility by over 16,000 sq. ft. to meet local demand.
data.description: A company situated in Winter Park has increased its premium climate-controlled space by over 16,000 square feet in response to increasing demand in the area. This latest expansion project, located in Winter Park, Florida, was officially opened on February 12, 2026, by Demetree Global. The new addition is a state-of-the-art facility aimed at meeting the growing market needs.

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. to release 2025 annual report on Feb 28, 2026, at 8:00 am ET, online at www.berkshirehathaway.com.
data.description: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. will release its 2025 Annual Report online on Saturday, February 28, 2026, around 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time at www.berkshirehathaway.com. Alongside the Annual Report, an earnings release will be published, featuring Greg Abel's inaugural annual letter to shareholders, detailing Berkshire's financial status and outcomes.

data.title: Financial expert Konda Bowling compares credit unions and banks for money storage in HelloNation article.
data.description: In Lawrenceburg, Tennessee, on February 12, 2026, an article questions the merits of keeping money in a credit union versus a bank. Financial Expert Konda Bowling from Employee Resources Credit Union shares insights.
